tasks.criterion: config.json schema extended with regression fields: .harness/config.json contains a regression object with keys enabled (boolean) and fail_fast (boolean). Values may be literal booleans or null (to signal "use default").

.harness/regression/ directory and README exist: The path .harness/regression/ exists as a directory, contains a regression.json file that is valid JSON with a top-level tasks array (may be empty on initialization), and contains a README.md explaining what the directory is for.

regression.json entries match the tasks.json schema: Every entry in .harness/regression/regression.json's tasks array (when non-empty) contains the fields task_id, criterion, grader_type, weight, is_gate, verification_command, rubric_dimension, plus one Sprint-7-added field graduated_from_sprint (integer). An empty tasks array passes vacuously.
tasks.verification_command: jq -e '.tasks | all(has("task_id") and has("criterion") and has("grader_type") and has("weight") and has("is_gate") and has("verification_command") and has("rubric_dimension") and has("graduated_from_sprint"))' .harness/regression/regression.json

tasks.criterion: harness-sprint SKILL contains a Step 0.5 Regression Gate section: skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md contains a heading whose text matches Step 0.5 and includes the word Regression (case-insensitive). The section must be positioned between Step 0 and Step 1.

Step 0.5 reads regression.json and runs each verification_command: The Step 0.5 section in skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md explicitly references the path .harness/regression/regression.json, iterating the tasks array and executing each task's verification_command, aborting the sprint when any verification_command fails and regression.fail_fast is true, and writing a timestamped results file under .harness/regression/runs/.
tasks.verification_command: grep -q 'regression/regression.json' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md && grep -q 'verification_command' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md && grep -q 'fail_fast' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md && grep -q 'regression/runs' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md

tasks.criterion: harness-summary writes graduated criteria into regression.json: skills/harness-summary/SKILL.md documents that saturated criteria (first-round-pass for 3+ consecutive sprints) are appended to .harness/regression/regression.json as machine-readable entries, not merely listed as recommendations in the summary markdown. Graduation is append-only.

evaluator.md notes thinking effort for regression vs capability: agents/evaluator.md contains a note stating that regression-criterion evaluation uses a lower thinking effort (medium) while fresh capability-criterion evaluation uses a higher effort (high or max). Sprint 8 wiring point.
tasks.verification_command: grep -qi 'regression' agents/evaluator.md && grep -qiE 'effort|thinking' agents/evaluator.md && grep -qiE 'medium' agents/evaluator.md && grep -qiE 'high|max' agents/evaluator.md

tasks.criterion: harness-conventions.md documents the regression.json schema: rules/harness-conventions.md contains a section describing regression.json — its role (append-only graduation output), its schema (the same fields as tasks.json plus graduated_from_sprint), and the gate semantics.
tasks.verification_command: grep -q 'regression.json' rules/harness-conventions.md && grep -q 'graduated_from_sprint' rules/harness-conventions.md && grep -qi 'append-only\|append only' rules/harness-conventions.md

tasks.criterion: sprint-07.tasks.json is emitted: .harness/contracts/sprint-07.tasks.json exists, is valid JSON, has a top-level sprint: 7 field and a tasks array with one entry per criterion in this contract (including gate criteria).
tasks.verification_command: jq -e '.sprint == 7 and (.tasks | length) >= 12 and (.tasks | all(has("task_id") and has("grader_type") and has("weight") and has("is_gate")))' .harness/contracts/sprint-07.tasks.json

tasks.criterion: Backward compatibility is explicit and correct: The updated skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md Step 0.5 section makes it unambiguous that a project whose .harness/regression/regression.json does not exist — or whose tasks array is empty — experiences no behavior change from Phase 1. The config knobs regression.enabled and regression.fail_fast must each have a documented default.

Regression graduation semantics are clear: A reader of the updated skills/harness-summary/SKILL.md saturation section should be able to explain (a) the graduation trigger, (b) which fields are copied vs added (graduated_from_sprint), (c) why graduation is append-only.

Fail-fast rationale is justified, not just asserted: The skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md Step 0.5 section should explain why regression failure must abort before contract negotiation rather than continuing as a warning.

Regression is positioned as the output of saturation, not a parallel system: Documentation makes clear that regression.json is the downstream product of existing saturation detection logic, with Sprint 6 tasks.json as the source of record.

No silent behavior change for pre-Phase-2 configs: A project whose .harness/config.json lacks the regression object and whose repository has no .harness/regression/regression.json file must run the harness identically to Phase 1.
No modification of prior sprint artifacts: .harness/evals/sprint-0[1-6]* and .harness/contracts/sprint-0[1-6]* must be unchanged from HEAD.
No ad-hoc regression commands — only tasks.json verification commands execute. Step 0.5 must run verbatim the verification_command string recorded on each task in regression.json.
